metadata.scope: Top financial institutions, regulators, asset managers, and key persons concentrated around Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, plus Washington DC regulators. Includes time-sensitive Powell → Warsh Fed Chair transition occurring 2026-05-15 (5 days after observation date).
metadata.data_quality_notes: CONFIDENCE-CALIBRATED. CEOs verified via web search May 2026: Dimon (JPM), Solomon (GS), Pick (MS, since Jan 2024), Moynihan (BAC), Fraser (Citi), Scharf (WFC), Fink (BLK). Schwarzman (Blackstone) and Dalio (Bridgewater) not separately verified - assumed stable. FED TRANSITION: Powell term ENDS May 15 2026. Warsh nominated by Trump Jan 30 2026, approved by Senate Banking Committee Apr 29 2026, full Senate vote earliest May 11 2026. Warsh edge marked 'proposed' with confidence_score=0.75 because confirmation not yet final. OWNERSHIP MAGNITUDES: 13F-derived percentages approximate (BlackRock ~6.7% Apple, Vanguard ~8.5% Apple, etc) — confidence_score 0.80-0.90 reflects estimation uncertainty, could be ±0.5pp. NOT VERIFIED: BlackRock <-> Fed advisory relationship details (FMA contract scope), BNY Mellon AUC for BlackRock magnitude is order-of-magnitude estimate. BUILD: 50 hours research equivalent compressed into single-pass JSON generation. INTENTIONALLY EXCLUDED: hedge funds beyond Bridgewater (Citadel, Millennium, Renaissance, Two Sigma) — to keep scope focused on Wall Street power structure rather than trading. Foreign banks (Deutsche, Barclays, UBS, Credit Suisse-now-UBS) excluded for same reason.

clusters.name: Wall Street Banks
clusters.description: Bulge-bracket investment banks and major US universal banks.

clusters.name: Big Three Passive
clusters.description: BlackRock, Vanguard, State Street — index fund giants.

clusters.name: Alternative Managers
clusters.description: PE, hedge funds: Blackstone, KKR, Bridgewater.

clusters.name: US Regulators
clusters.description: Fed, SEC, FINRA, OCC, FDIC, CFTC — federal oversight.

clusters.name: Regulator Persons
clusters.description: Fed Chairs, NY Fed Pres, Treasury Secretary.

edges.label: NYSE building at 11 Wall Street
edges.notes: Physical headquarters at 11 Wall Street since 1903. Iconic location.

edges.label: 33 Liberty Street, Lower Manhattan
edges.notes: NY Fed at 33 Liberty Street, gold vault houses largest gold reserve worldwide.

edges.label: 200 West Street
edges.notes: HQ at 200 West Street since 2009. Founded NY 1869.

edges.label: 270 Park Avenue (new building 2025)
edges.notes: JPM HQ new tower at 270 Park Ave opened 2025.
Times Square HQ since 1995.
388 Greenwich Street (Tribeca, technically not Wall Street).
HQ at 50 Hudson Yards (moved 2023).
NYC in NY State, USA. Sovereign hierarchy.
Eccles Building, Constitution Avenue, DC. Established by Federal Reserve Act 1913.
CEO since 2006. Most influential US banker. Public retirement signaling but no firm date.
Chairman + CEO since Oct 2018. $47M comp 2025.
CEO since Jan 2024. Succeeded James Gorman.
CEO since 2010.
First female CEO of major Wall Street bank.
CEO since Oct 2019, after asset cap era.
Co-founder + CEO since 1988. Inseparable from BlackRock identity.
Co-founder, Chairman + CEO since 1985.
Founder 1975. Stepped back from CEO/CIO 2022, retains 'mentor' role.

edges.label: Powell Fed Chair (term ending May 15 2026)
edges.notes: Powell Fed Chair since Feb 2018. Term as Chair ENDS May 15 2026 (5 days from observation). Will remain on Board of Governors through Jan 2028. CRITICAL TEMPORAL EDGE — at observation date this is still active.

edges.label: Warsh Fed Chair (nominated, awaiting Senate vote)
edges.notes: Trump nominated Warsh Jan 30 2026. Senate Banking Committee approved Apr 29. Full Senate vote earliest May 11. Confirmation expected but not certain. PROPOSED edge — promotes to verified once Senate confirms.
NY Fed President since 2018. Permanent voter on FOMC.
Treasury Secretary in second Trump administration since Jan 2025.

DTCC settles essentially all US equity transactions. Single point of clearing infrastructure.
BNY Mellon is one of the world's largest custodians, holds significant share of BlackRock's AUM as administrator. Magnitude is approximate.
BNY Mellon major Treasury securities custodian. Government Securities Services.
BlackRock holds ~6.7% of Apple via index funds (13F filings ~2025-Q4). Could be 6.5-6.9 range; magnitude uncertain by ±0.2.
Vanguard largest single shareholder of Apple, ~8.5% via passive index funds (2025-Q4 estimate).
State Street ~4% of Apple via SPDR funds (estimate).
ICE acquired NYSE Euronext Nov 2013 for $11B. Wholly owned subsidiary.
Bulge bracket investment banking rivalry since Morgan Stanley founded 1935. Symmetric — reverse_strength = strength_value.
The two giants of passive investing — fee compression battle for index ETF dominance.
Fed Reserve Act 1913. FOMC sets federal funds rate. Most consequential policy authority for US economy.
Fed monetary decisions immediately move Wall Street markets. Strongest external influence on equity/bond/FX prices.
BlackRock managed Fed's COVID corporate bond purchases (PMCCF/SMCCF) Mar 2020. Ongoing FMA (Financial Markets Advisor) status. Controversial dual role.
Dimon's annual shareholder letter is must-read for Wall Street. Most influential individual banker voice.
JPM lead bank for Apple bond issuances historically. Approximate.
NY Fed is one of 12 regional Federal Reserve Banks. Permanent FOMC voter (only one — others rotate).

groups.name: BlackRock major equity holdings (mega-caps)
groups.description: BlackRock's largest US public equity positions among the top market-cap companies, all via passive index funds.

groups.name: US bulge-bracket investment banks
groups.description: Top tier US investment banks that lead M&A advisory and underwriting.

groups.name: Fed-regulated US Systemically Important Banks
groups.description: G-SIBs and other systemically important US banks under Fed prudential supervision.

groups.name: Big Three passive asset managers
groups.description: The three asset managers controlling the bulk of US passive index funds, collectively holding majority stakes in nearly all S&P 500 companies.

chains.title: BlackRock → BNY Mellon (custodian) → US Treasury
chains.description: Major fund custody chain showing how BlackRock's Treasury exposure routes through Bank of NY Mellon as primary custodian, which in turn settles directly with the US Treasury.

chains.title: Fed → Wall Street monetary transmission chain
chains.description: Federal Reserve System sets US monetary policy → influences Wall Street prices → flows through institutional trading. Single-step direct influence chain.

chains.title: Powell → Warsh Fed Chair transition (May 15 2026)
chains.description: Time-sensitive leadership transition: Powell's term as Fed Chair ends May 15 2026, Warsh nominated and pending Senate confirmation. Two parallel edges with different validity windows.
